gauge.name: Knife River (MN) near Two Harbors

impacts.statement: Water goes over the Church Road (County Road 102) bridge and indundates portions of Scenic Highway 61 downstream of the intersection with County Road 102. Extensive flooding of residential yards with water encroaching on riverside homes in the town of Knife River.
Water may begin to inundate low lying areas of residential properties on Alger Smith Road (County Road 103).
Bottom of the stairs to the rest station trail become inundated and portions of the Knife River hiking trail on the south side of the river are under water.
Lowest spots of the Knife River hiking trail on the south side of the river begin to be inundanted.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States

hydronotes.statement: Gauge reading may be affected by ice.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
